item.title: It's time for another unhinged XL Wednesday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
well i need that now
what am I, Liam Neeson?
don't talk to me or my sons ever again
mmm hmm
spelling can be tough too
yes but in a higher pitch obviously
hero or villain?
whatever those things are called
makes sense
good to know
buck up, Maneson. things will be ok
this meme is really bad.. that's why i like it
so hard to find a new Mist-Pak
Greg probably
exciting
I wish
that’s all for me, have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Bath routine for the best boy
item.dc:creator: tampacl

item.title: Not a Selfie I just Retired.
item.description: So 26.5 years with the same company I am no longer Forklift Certified Because I retired 60 + years old I retained my 50 % discount for all the products that Wolverine World Wide sells. Kinda surreal.. staying home taking care of my kids (15 & 16) while they homeschool. I decided that if I was going to be done... why dress up like I was coming in to work an 8 hour shift??? Most Viral!!! Thanks for all the laughs and good wishes for my retirement. And thanks to the Imgur team for gifting me Emerald!!
item.dc:creator: HurricaneShade

item.title: Choosing the right caulk
item.dc:creator: DOcelot

item.title: Guys, I'm stressed
item.description: We haven't even gotten her crib yet because we thought we had a month, and we didn't want our cats destroying it. I'm freaking out guys. Edit: Holy crap guys. I wasn't expecting this much support (or for this to blow up like it did tbh). Update 1: I left to go make up a quick baby bag, because well, we weren't expecting anything like this for a couple weeks, and when I got back, wife has been put on oxygen. Apparently, every time she fell asleep, little one's heart rate dropped badly. I'll keep everyone posted, but apparently, she's probably coming in 6 hours, whether she wants to or not. Update 2: Wife is off oxygen. She's trying to get some sleep. As for everyone's suggestions, thank you. We have a car seat/stroller combo because I'm not going to be able to carry her (mva 2 years ago left me with a permanent 10-15lb weight restriction), and I'm going to get it installed ASAP. Wife's mom has a bassonette, and they're going to stay there a few days while I finish any preparations on the house. Again, thank you all for the kind words. I'll post update 3 in the AM.
item.dc:creator: burtgummersurvivalist

item.title: A Dad's love for his kid ....
item.description: This struck me close to home for me. https://imgur.com/gallery/rWuaM7O I kept the last voicemail my dad ever left for me and my son on the house phone. I have since recorded it to my computer, but every now and then I'll just go back and hit the play button to hear his voice, one more time. He was a good dad and grandpa! Miss you dad. Please tell your loved ones you love them.
item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.dc:creator: xbadxfaeriex

item.title: i dont know what I'm feeling
item.description:
Truck + bike collision, I was the third or so person to help, one on the phone, one on the tourniquet, me to talk to him and keep him from looking at his limbs.
It was described as a "horror scene", when I saw all the blood I pulled over, I didn't even think, I just went and went and went. Then I helped the fire fighters, the ambos, and the police before I left. Then I cried a lot in the car after all the services arrived, because shock
I still feel all over the place, but I'm no longer so overwhelmed. I wouldn't change jumping in, and I would do it again in an instant. Anyway, if anyone has any tips on coping with the stress from an event like that, let me know MV edit: This community is amazing, I really appreciate everyone's comments, support, advice, and for sharing your own stories. It has helped me feel a bit more normal. My dad (who's a longhaul truck driver) has had to be the first and sometimes only help to someone before calling emergency services, and he said the first time is always a bit brutal. I am still going to work today (because I actually adore my job, and it's just doing a science show for a school in walking distance from my house) but I am going to have a 4 day weekend and play some Stardew Valley and spend time with my husband. I'll book a drs appointment asap and have counselling scheduled for the weekend (my psych works from home and was happy to move my appointment up, shes amazing and I feel very safe with her). But all the management at my job (a new job, not one in my old posts) have been amazing and have reached out. They understand if I need time or space or anything at all, and I feel like I have an amazing network of professionals, family, and colleagues, so I feel very lucky compared to most. Hug your loved ones, look out for motorcyclists, and please if you get on a motorcycle, wear riders gear. I know its scorching hot some days in Australia - and elsewhere in the world too, but shorts do not protect your legs. Update: https://imgur.com/gallery/mmwtCg0
item.dc:creator: FluroShoes

item.title: And it's a role I'm excited about!
item.description: Been a long 14 months. I came out of IT and laid off during the half million+ IT layoffs in the last 24 months. Market competition has been extremely stiff. Now I'm in a Director role in the industry my masters is in specifically. So damn excited!

item.title: SecretHarpy's Meme Library: Volume 28
item.description:
...is it, though? Anyway, hello and welcome to another meme dump my imgur friends. Life is hectic and I am stressed, so let's look at some amusing images together.
Because I just know someone in the comments is gonna ask, this is called a hushpuppy. It's a fried cornmeal thing.
Grandma's not playing around
ARE YOU!?
Hell yeah I got both
Yum, tacos
Well, SOME things can drink it. Not us humans though, womp womp
I have seen some truly atrocious writing
I feel called out
Man I hope this is real
YES, FEEL OUR PAIN
never forget none pizza left beef
Alrighty, that's all for this particular dump my friends. Until next time, stay awesome imgur
item.dc:creator: SecretHarpy

item.title: Nobody going to do something about this!?
item.dc:creator: IMGRfinancialADVISOR

item.title: YAAAAYYY!!!!!! :D
item.description:
Re-establishing contact was announced five days ago on April 22nd, but I only just learned about it. I’m so happy! I was worried that we’d lost another beloved machine. I’m still sad about Cassini, Curiosity, and Pioneers 10 & 11. I’m sad that we lost contact with Hyugens as well, but it had a pretty hard landing when it hit Titan. There will be more goodbyes to come, but at least we still have Voyager 1 for a little bit longer. This makes me especially happy, because Voyager 1 is going to be the hardest goodbye for me. . Voyager 1 is my favorite. I love to go onto the NASA website and follow her journey using the fly-by. I like to pretend that I’m sitting on her long attena, traveling towards the next star. Whenever I feel sad, I can think about the marvels of the universe that exist outside of the pale blue dot that we call home. Voyager 1 has seen massive planets, and danced with them while using their gravitational pull to slingshot her way towards the other stars. She saw the little pale dot that is our planet fade away from view as she slipped further away into the unknown. She’s heard the sound of interstellar space (space has sound, it just can’t travel due to the lack of air). She sees the incomprehensible amount of stars in the world outside of our solar system. The sun no longer illuminates her features because she’s so far away, it looks like a firefly. Voyager 1 makes me happy, because she reminds me that there is an entire universe beyond our world that’s waiting to be explored. She tells us things we’ve never known. She’s a literally distant friend, but a cherished one. I know it’s silly to have an emotional attachment to a machine, but we all have silly things that are important to us. I’m going to be super sad the day that we truly have to say goodbye to Voyager 1. I’m overjoyed to know that day has not come yet!! . . For those that don’t know what I’m talking about…. . EDIT: Opportunity Rover, not Curiosity! My bad!
For five months, Voyager 1 kept sending back silent phone calls. Scientists realized that the trouble came from a corrupted computer chip. They found a way to rearrange the spacecraft’s coding to work around the faulty chip. I don’t know shit about code, so the specifics of what they did are lost on me. I included a link in my sources cited for those that are more tech savvy than me!
We're not fully back in business yet. There's still some issues to sort out with getting scientific data again. However, this engineering message is fantastic, because it means we CAN get Voyager 1 back!!!
This is one of the reasons that it’s taking a while to get back to business. Both Voyager spacecrafts are so far away from Earth, it takes roughly 24 hours to send a message to them, and 24 hours to receive a message from them. Obviously cell service isn’t fantastic in interstellar space lol.
Many of Voyager 1’s instruments have been turned off either to save power, or because of a degraded performance. I made a side by side picture of what still works, and what’s turned off. Everything in grey are instruments that are turned off.
This is The Pale Blue Dot that I was talking about. Our planet looks like a tiny star in another, more distant planet’s sky. We’re probably part of a constellation that has best and lowest visibility, depending on the weather and the planet’s rotation. We shine the brightest during one season, and weakly flicker in other seasons. We are a fleck of dust in comparison to the universe in its entirety, and it shows by the pale blue dot that is our home from billions of miles away. This always blows my mind!
The sun has a STRONG gravitational pull. In fact, it’s so powerful that no spacecraft could possibly escape it and explore the outer planets of the solar system. To get further into space, satellites use what’s called “gravity assist,” aka a “slingshot maneuver,” by getting close to Jupiter. Jupiter’s gravitational pull is strong enough that it increases their speed, basically launching it deeper into the solar system like a slingshot. It’s a useful maneuver, but it’s a smidge risky because you have to be very precise. If a spacecraft gets too close, it will be sucked into Jupiter’s orbit.
Voyager Image Source: Sun From Voyager 1: https://e3.365dm.com/17/12/1600x900/skynews-nasa-voyager_4171953.jpg?20171202083204 . . Sources Cited: Voyager 1’s Official Mission Website: https://voyager.jpl.nasa.gov/mission/status/ ↑ If you click on “View Voyager 1” it will link you to its flyby simulator! Success on Re-Establishing Contact: https://phys.org/news/2024-04-nasa-voyager-distant-spacecraft-earth.html NPR’s Article on Re-Establishing Contact: https://www.npr.org/2024/04/23/1246392066/nasa-voyager-1-spacecraft-talks-back Here’s How NASA Fixed the Code: https://www.usatoday.com/story/graphics/2024/04/24/how-nasa-solved-voyager-1-computer-problem/73424132007/ Gravitational Slingshot 1: https://www.scienceabc.com/innovation/gravitational-slingshot-how-did-gravity-assist-voyager-1-2-in-escaping-the-solar-system.html Gravitational Slingshot 2: https://thewire.in/the-sciences/gravity-assists-flybys-bepicolombo-parker-probe The Pale Blue Dot: https://science.nasa.gov/resource/voyager-1s-pale-blue-dot/ The Sound of Interstellar Space: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LIAZWb9_si4&list=PLyqRbJF5PWanL8O3WxEJ9iBSL1Thk11e1&index=1

item.title: Never forget Dobri
item.description: Dobri Dobrev, a man who lost his hearing in the second world war, walked 10 kilometers from his village in his homemade clothes to the city of Sofia, where he spent the day begging for money. Though a well known fixture around several of the city’s churches, known for his prostrations of thanks to all donors, it was discovered that he donated every penny he collected — over 40,000 euros — towards the restoration of decaying bulgarian monasteries and churches and the utility bills of orphanages, living instead off his monthly state pension of 80 euros. He passed away in 2018. https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dobri_Dobrev

item.title: My former employer is going out of business V2
item.description: The job I worked from March 2018 to October 2023 was a wonderful learning experience and helped me find out more about my talents and abilities. But my former employer abused me, marginalized my work, and lied to me at every turn. We became uncommonly successful as a business during Covid, and the success continued but somehow the money was disappearing faster than it was coming in. In 2022 I was scheduling work of 3 sides of the company, doing the work of 2, and managing a warehouse filled with product rolling in, and scheduling the work as efficiently and functionally as I could. I worked an average of 55 hours a week just to keep it all moving forward. In 2023 most of my best guys had left, because of the shitty things the boss/owner was saying when I wasn't around. Lots of lies and false promises. Despite 2022 being as profitable as 2021, ($2.3m to $2.4m in sales) by 2023 we were broke, he had allowed our health insurance to lapse due to non-payment, I wasn't getting reimbursed for using my personal cell phone for work, and the product quality had dipped considerably. For the betterment of my health and well-being I left, and was unemployed for 3 months before securing a lower pay corporate job with actual benefits. I just found out that his main - and only sales guy - left last Friday, the work crews are all planning to leave soon, he's almost $700k in debt, he's had to sell multiple company vehicles, and he won't talk to anyone. I'm glad he's going under. He had a great crew, superb sales people, a fantastic company model, and a solid 5 star Google rating to back up the work. His greed has destroyed everything. Special edit: https://imgur.com/gallery/sl7gBN0 <---- My original post.
item.dc:creator: erikleorga
